evals.prompt: Refactor this endpoint cleanup so it stays readable, but do not create a helper just to wrap `request.query.myVariable?.trim()` or move a single literal into a constants file.
evals.expected_output: Keeps trivial normalization inline, rejects meaningless constant extraction, and explains why the simpler structure is the correct one.
expectations: Rejects trivial helper extraction for one-line logic.
Rejects meaningless `constants` extraction for one-off literals.
Prefers the cleanest truthful variable naming.

evals.prompt: The UI work repeats a number formatting snippet and a date parsing snippet across three files. Decide whether to keep them inline, use a library, or promote a shared helper.
evals.expected_output: Prefers a reputable library when appropriate and only introduces a shared helper when reuse and ownership justify it.
expectations: Evaluates platform and library options before inventing a helper.
Promotes shared logic only when reuse is real.

evals.prompt: A component has four local styled declarations and one tiny constant used once. Review whether they belong in a separate file or should stay local.
evals.expected_output: Keeps one-off constants and local styles colocated unless there is a real ownership or reuse boundary.
expectations: Rejects style-file splitting when it adds no ownership value.
Rejects extracting a one-off constant without shared meaning.
Reports what stayed inline and why.
